sourceMovie.title: Wanted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Wanted (2008) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Wanted — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Mechanic
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Mechanic (2011) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Wanted rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Extraction 2 (2023) is the kind of film Wanted fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Long Kiss Goodnight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Long Kiss Goodnight shares Wanted's gift for action — the 1996 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Shade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Wanted compelling — the action, the thriller — shows up in Shade (2003) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Bullet Train
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Wanted is alive in Bullet Train (2022), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Commuter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Commuter (2018) operates in the same action as Wanted, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Road to Perdition (2002) belongs to the same conversation as Wanted — both anchor their thriller in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Hitman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wanted's action stayed with you, Hitman (2007) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Brave One
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Wanted excels in thriller, The Brave One (2007) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wanted?
faq.answer: Try John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum, The Mechanic, Extraction 2—each shares DNA with Wanted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wanted?
faq.answer: Wanted (2008) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wanted worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
